title: The OSI and TCP/IP Protocol Suite Models
description: A side-by-side comparison of the 7-layer OSI Model and the 4-layer TCP/IP Model, showing the encapsulation process and how protocols map between the two.
relationships: OSI Model (7 Layers) is a conceptual framework, TCP/IP Model (4 Layers) is the practical implementation
Data is encapsulated as it moves down the stack (e.g., Data -> Segment -> Packet -> Frame -> Bits)
The TCP/IP Application Layer maps to the top three OSI layers
The TCP/IP Network Access Layer maps to the bottom two OSI layers
